sections.paragraph:
αἰώνιος, α, ον [αἰών]

—1. ‘relating to a period of time extending far into the past’, long ages ago Ro 16:25. In 2 Ti 1:9; Tit 1:2 the prep. πρό invites the rendering before time began.

—2. ‘relating to time without boundaries or interruption’, eternal Ro 16:26; Hb 9:14.

—3. relating to a period of unending duration’, permanent, lasting Lk 16:9; 2 Cor 5:1; Hb 5:9.
